summary: Recent updates in the 2D graphics area of Firefox, particularly regarding how the browser handles text and fonts, have introduced important improvements. The changes facilitate better rendering of SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics) glyphs by ensuring that font drawing parameters are correctly passed, enhancing the clarity and quality of displayed text. Additionally, the adjustments to FreeType2, a popular font rendering library, improve how the browser manages font metrics such as ex-height and cap-height, resulting in more accurate text representation. Overall, these updates contribute to a **smoother and more visually appealing experience when viewing text in web pages**, especially in graphics-intensive applications.
